policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released binding climate risk management guidelines requiring SCBs with assets above ₹10,000 crore to conduct annual climate stress tests beginning FY2027-28. Banks must integrate physical and transition risk metrics into ICAAP submissions within 18 months.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting for top 500 companies by market cap effective FY2026-27, expanding from previous top 150 threshold. New requirements include Scope 3 emissions disclosure and water intensity metrics with mandatory third-party assurance.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Guidelines on Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI issued draft regulations allowing insurers to file parametric products tied to weather indices including rainfall, temperature, and cyclone wind speed. Guidelines specify minimum correlation thresholds of 0.7 between index triggers and actual losses for product approval.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Disclosure Requirements
policy.description: BCBS published final Pillar 3 climate disclosure framework requiring internationally active banks to disclose climate-related financial risks, including portfolio-level emissions and physical risk exposures, by January 2028.

policy.title: EU Commission Adopts Additional Taxonomy Technical Screening Criteria for Emerging Market Activities
policy.description: European Commission expanded EU Taxonomy to include technical screening criteria for agriculture and manufacturing activities common in emerging markets. New criteria recognize transition pathways for high-emission sectors in developing economies.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated climate scenarios incorporating sub-national physical risk data for emerging markets including India, with district-level projections for flooding, heat stress, and water scarcity through 2100.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide climate risk analytics for the bank's commercial real estate and infrastructure lending portfolio. Deal reportedly valued at $4M annually over three years.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A for ESG SaaS Platform Expansion
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed Series A led by Sequoia India to expand its ESG compliance platform targeting mid-market Indian enterprises. Company claims 200+ enterprise customers and plans to add climate risk modules.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Climate Risk Scores
competitors.description: Moody's released climate risk scores covering 50,000+ Indian corporate and sub-sovereign entities, incorporating monsoon variability, heat stress, and flood exposure at asset-level granularity.

competitors.title: XDI Expands Physical Risk Database to Cover Indian District-Level Assets
competitors.description: Australian climate analytics firm XDI extended its Gross Physical Risk assessment to cover all 766 Indian districts, enabling asset-level physical risk scoring for buildings and infrastructure.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Partners with Federal Bank for Agri-Climate Products
competitors.description: Kerala-based CarbonMint signed partnership with Federal Bank to integrate its blockchain-based carbon credit verification into agricultural lending products, targeting 100,000 farmer accounts.

competitors.title: Watershed Raises $150M Series D, Announces Emerging Markets Expansion
competitors.description: US-based carbon management platform raised at $2.5B valuation with plans to enter India and Southeast Asia. Company announced partnerships with three Indian IT services firms for Scope 3 tracking.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: A Stress Testing Framework
research.description: RBI working paper proposes methodology for translating physical hazard exposures into PD/LGD adjustments, finding that a 2°C scenario could increase NPAs by 1.8-3.2 percentage points for agriculture-heavy portfolios by 2035.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience in South Asia
research.description: Report identifies $15B annual protection gap for climate perils in India and recommends index-based products for flood, drought, and cyclone coverage. Highlights basis risk as key barrier requiring granular hazard data.

research.title: Climate-Related Credit Risk: Analytical Approaches for Emerging Market Banks
research.description: IFC framework document details approaches for integrating physical and transition risk into credit assessment for EM banks, recommending sector-specific vulnerability coefficients and forward-looking scenario analysis.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Challenges and Solutions
research.description: UNEPFI study finds 67% of EM companies lack adequate environmental disclosure compared to 23% in developed markets. Report recommends alternative data approaches including satellite imagery and supply chain proxies.

research.title: Sigma Report: Natural Catastrophe Losses in 2025 and Implications for Asia
research.description: Swiss Re reports $142B global insured nat-cat losses in 2025, with India experiencing record $8.2B in insured losses from monsoon flooding and cyclones. Uninsured losses estimated at 4x insured amount.

research.title: State of Climate Finance in India 2025
research.description: CPI tracks $68B in climate finance flows to India in FY2024-25, noting 78% directed to mitigation versus adaptation. Report identifies ₹11 trillion annual investment gap for India's 2030 climate targets.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate stress test mandate creates a 18-month window where banks will pay for credible PD/LGD models—StepChange must capture this demand before global players localize.
body: The signals this fortnight are unambiguous: Indian climate risk is graduating from voluntary disclosure to prudential requirement. RBI's final guidelines, Basel's Pillar 3 framework, and IRDAI's parametric draft together form a regulatory triptych that validates all three of StepChange's strategic bets simultaneously. The policy momentum is no longer aspirational—it's operational.

RBI's April 2nd guidelines are the most consequential Indian climate policy since BRSR. The ₹10,000 crore asset threshold captures roughly 50 scheduled commercial banks, each of which now needs climate stress testing capability within 18 months. The RBI working paper's specific finding—that physical risk could add 1.8-3.2 percentage points to NPAs—gives us a quantitative anchor for sales conversations. StepChange should immediately develop a stress testing module aligned with RBI's ICAAP integration requirement; this is not a nice-to-have, it's a compliance mandate with a deadline.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership is a warning shot. At $4M annually, global players are pricing India enterprise deals aggressively, and HDFC's choice signals that top-tier banks will default to recognized global brands unless domestic alternatives demonstrate superior local data. Meanwhile, Moody's 50,000-entity India coverage and XDI's district-level expansion show that competitors are racing to solve the India granularity problem. StepChange's edge must be speed-to-compliance and regulatory intimacy—we understand RBI's requirements faster than Jupiter can localize.

The World Bank's parametric insurance report quantifying a $15B annual protection gap and identifying basis risk as the key barrier is a strategic gift. This directly validates our parametric insurance bet and points to our differentiation: granular hazard data that minimizes basis risk. We should proactively share this report with IRDAI during their consultation period and position StepChange's data as the infrastructure that makes their new guidelines implementable.

Watch for: RBI's response to Basel Pillar 3 finalization—expect a consultation paper on disclosure harmonization by Q3 2026. Action item: Schedule meetings with risk heads at 10 SCBs in the ₹10,000-50,000 crore asset band this month—these mid-tier banks lack in-house climate risk capability and face the same deadline as SBI.
